  "context_message_length": 23500,
  "latest_summary": [
    "## OBJECTIVE\nThe user is building **Crysknife** (`crys`), a personal multi-agent orchestrator for managing 6-15 kiro-cli agents in tmux. It's a simplified, learner-friendly version of Steve Yegge's Gas Town, written in Go. The broader goal is progressing from Stage 5 (single agent) through Stage 7 (Crysknife) and understanding the path to Stage 8 (Gas Town). Two core documents drive the project: `crysknife-design.md` (the build spec) and `multi-agent-progression.md` (the personal roadmap).\n\n## USER GUIDANCE\n- Go for implementation, TypeScript/Python if needed\n- Learn by building, not copy Gas Town\n- Values traceability (AC → properties → tasks → tests) from existing spec-driven workflow\n- Keeps `.kiro/specs/` structure — evolves it, doesn't replace it\n- Adaptive planning (living plan.md), not rigid phases\n- CLI shortcut is `crys`, project name is Crysknife (Dune reference)\n- No emojis in documents\n- Use consistent terminology across docs — Crysknife terms, with Gas Town equivalents in parentheses where helpful\n- When proposing two options, pick the more reliable one as primary and mention the other as secondary\n- Write minimal code — only what directly contributes to the solution\n- Fan of Tolkien and Dune for naming/theming\n\n## COMPLETED\n\n### Major Design Evolution in This Session\nThe design doc underwent a significant upgrade when we discovered kiro-cli's native features (agent configs, hooks, MCP servers, context resources). This changed the architecture from \"fight the tool with tmux send-keys\" to \"work with kiro-cli natively.\"\n\n### Documents Modified This Session\n\n1. **`docs/crysknife-design.md`** — Major updates:\n   - Added design decision #13: kiro-cli native integration\n   - Updated decision #4: agents are kiro-cli agent configs (was \"kiro-cli sessions\")\n   - Updated decision #10: agents interact through MCP tools (was \"write to task files only\")\n   - **New section: \"kiro-cli Integration\"** (before Architecture) covering:\n     - Agent configs: mayor.json, worker-N.json (generated by `crys start`), merger.json — full JSON examples\n     - Primary approach: per-worker generated configs (each needs different task file, area, branch). Alternative noted: single shared worker.json with dynamic agentSpawn hook\n     - Hooks mapping: agentSpawn=GUPP, preToolUse=area enforcement (exit code 2 blocks), stop=heartbeat\n     - Area boundary enforcement script (`enforce-area.sh`) with full bash implementation\n     - MCP server: `crys mcp-serve` with role-based tool filtering table (8 tools across 3 roles)\n     - Context resources: what each role auto-loads\n     - Full \"How It All Fits Together\" walkthrough (startup → during work → task complete)\n   - Updated Agent Detection: heartbeat via stop hooks (primary), pane diffing (fallback)\n   - Updated restart sequence: `kiro-cli chat --agent <id>`, config auto-loads everything\n   - New CLI commands: `crys heartbeat`, `crys my-task`, `crys mcp-serve`\n   - Updated `crys init` output: includes hooks/, agent configs\n   - Updated `crys start`: generates per-worker agent configs, launches with `--agent`\n   - Updated `crys sling`: regenerates worker agent config with new area restrictions\n   - Updated `crys done`: primary invocation is MCP tool `@crysknife/done`\n   - Updated all templates: `crys done {{AGENT_ID}}` → `@crysknife/done` MCP tool calls\n   - Updated mayor template: tools section uses `@crysknife/*` MCP tools\n   - Updated Data Flow diagram: heartbeat-based monitoring, agent config regeneration\n   - Updated System Overview diagram: added `.kiro/agents/` and hooks directory\n   - Updated Project Structure: new files (heartbeat.go, mytask.go, mcpserve.go, agentcfg/, mcp/, hooks/)\n   - Updated Implementation Phases: Phase 1 includes agent config generation, Phase 2 includes MCP server\n   - Updated Gas Town mapping: GUPP → agentSpawn + stop hooks, gt sling → crys sling + @crysknife/sling\n   - **Added \"Future: State Storage Upgrade Path\"** section: JSON → SQLite → Dolt (what Beads uses)\n\n2. **`docs/multi-agent-progression.md`** — Major updates:\n   - Stage overview: Stage 7 now \"6-15 agents, Crysknife orchestrator (with upgrades)\", Stage 8 \"20+ agents\"\n   - Concepts table: updated all Gas Town terms to Crysknife equivalents (Hooks→Task Files, Patrols→crys watch, Mail→Shared Files, gt sling→crys sling, GUPP Nudge→crys nudge, Shiny/Quick→full/standard/quick)\n   - Patrols section: rewritten to describe `crys watch` with pane diffing thresholds\n   - Similarity map: updated to Crysknife terms\n   - Adoption list: replaced hooks/patrols/mail with task files, crys watch, Git worktrees\n   - **Stage 7 completely rewritten**: references crysknife-design.md, includes kiro-cli native integration section, scaling/breaking points chart, updated CLI commands, implementation phases\n   - **New: Scaling Limits and Breaking Points chart** (6→8-10→10-12→12-15→15-20→20-25→25-30→30+) with what breaks and what to fix at each tier\n   - Role mapping table: workers \"4-12\", crys watch for Witness\n   - Context Preservation: updated with agent configs, hooks, MCP, preToolUse enforcement\n   - Timeline: added scaling milestones (8-10 at month 2-3, 10-12 at month 3-4)\n   - Key Principles: added #9 \"Work with the tool, not against it\" and #10 \"Scale by upgrading, not redesigning\"\n\n### Key Architectural Decisions Made This Session\n\n- **kiro-cli agent configs** replace tmux send-keys prompting. Each role (mayor, worker, merger) gets a `.kiro/agents/<role>.json` with prompt, tools, context, hooks, MCP server.\n- **Per-worker generated configs** (primary) vs single shared worker.json (secondary). Generated configs chosen because each worker needs different task file, area restrictions, and branch.\n- **Hooks as GUPP**: agentSpawn loads task on startup, stop hook updates heartbeat, preToolUse enforces area boundaries with exit code 2 blocking.\n- **MCP server** (`crys mcp-serve`): agents call `@crysknife/done`, `@crysknife/sling`, etc. as native tool calls. Role-based filtering (workers can't sling, mayor can't merge-done).\n- **Heartbeat-based detection** (primary) replaces pane content diffing (now fallback). Stop hook runs `crys heartbeat <id>` after each response.\n- **Beads uses Dolt** (not JSONL anymore). Dolt = MySQL with Git semantics. Each worker gets own Dolt branch, cell-level merge. This is the Stage 8+ upgrade path.\n- **Crysknife realistic ceiling**: ~12-15 agents with SQLite upgrade. Past 15, fundamental assumptions break (single mayor, single merger, hard area boundaries, you at keyboard).\n\n### Crysknife vs Gas Town Comparison\n- Crysknife better at: area enforcement (preToolUse hooks = hard guarantee), native tool integration (works with kiro-cli not against it), simplicity (3 roles vs 7), traceability\n- Gas Town better at: scale (20-30+), autonomy (runs overnight), workflow composition (MEOW stack), recovery (gt seance), battle-tested\n\n### Three Files Selected for New Crysknife Repo\n```\ncrysknife/docs/\n├── crysknife-design.md          # the blueprint\n├── gas-town-user-manual.md      # operational wisdom reference\n└── multi-agent-progression.md   # personal roadmap\n```\n`gastown.md` (first article) left behind — everything useful already distilled into the other docs.\n\n## TECHNICAL CONTEXT\n\n### File Locations\n- Project root: `/Users/lemi/Documents/code/projects/chip8-emulator`\n- OS: macOS\n- Design doc: `docs/crysknife-design.md` (~1594 lines)\n- Progression doc: `docs/multi-agent-progression.md` (~865 lines)\n- Gas Town user manual: `docs/gas-town-user-manual.md`\n\n### kiro-cli Features Discovered (Critical for Implementation)\nAgent configs stored in `.kiro/agents/<name>.json` (workspace) or `~/.kiro/agents/<name>.json` (global). Local takes precedence. Key fields:\n- `prompt`: inline or `file:///path` — system prompt\n- `tools`: available tools list\n- `allowedTools`: auto-approved tools (supports wildcards)\n- `toolsSettings`: per-tool config (`allowedPaths`, `deniedPaths` for fs_write)\n- `resources`: auto-loaded context files (`file://` always loaded, `skill://` on demand)\n- `hooks`: `agentSpawn`, `userPromptSubmit`, `preToolUse` (exit 2 = block), `postToolUse`, `stop`\n- `mcpServers`: MCP server configs per agent\n- `keyboardShortcut`: quick switch binding\n- `welcomeMessage`: shown on agent switch\n\nLaunch with: `kiro-cli chat --agent <name>`\n\nHook stdin receives JSON with `hook_event_name`, `cwd`, `tool_name`, `tool_input`. preToolUse exit code 2 blocks execution, STDERR returned to LLM.\n\nMCP servers configured per-agent or globally. Scope: default/workspace/global. `--agent` flag ties server to specific agent.\n\n### MCP Tools by Role (from design doc)\n```\n@crysknife/status       MAYOR+WORKER+MERGER   Read state.json\n@crysknife/done         WORKER only           Mark self done, notify mayor+merger\n@crysknife/feedback     WORKER+MERGER         Write to own task file Feedback section\n@crysknife/sling        MAYOR only            Assign work to worker\n@crysknife/queue        MAYOR only            Add/remove tasks from queue\n@crysknife/merge-queue  MERGER only           Read pending merge queue\n@crysknife/merge-done   MERGER only           Mark merge complete\n@crysknife/nudge        MAYOR only            Nudge stuck agent\n```\n\n### Crysknife Project Structure (Updated)\n```\ncrysknife/\n├── main.go\n├── cmd/ (init, start, stop, status, sling, queue, watch, nudge, done, convoy, heartbeat, mytask, mcpserve)\n├── internal/ (state/, tmux/, monitor/, template/, agentcfg/, mcp/)\n├── templates/ (mayor.md, worker-prompt.md, merger-prompt.md, full.md, standard.md, quick.md, principles.md)\n├── hooks/ (enforce-area.sh)\n├── go.mod, go.sum, README.md\n```\n\n### Implementation Phases (Updated)\n1. Foundation: init, start, stop, status, heartbeat, my-task, state read/write, tmux, worktrees, agent config generation\n2. Work + MCP: sling, queue, mcp-serve, MCP tools, templates, merger standing orders, enforce-area hook\n3. Monitoring: watch (heartbeat primary + pane diffing fallback), nudge, auto-nudge, auto-restart\n4. Planning: convoy, remaining MCP tools, plan file support, dashboard\n\n### Scaling Breaking Points\n6 (design target) → 8-10 (you're bottleneck, trust mayor) → 10-12 (tmux layout, use session groups) → 12-15 (JSON contention, upgrade SQLite) → 15-20 (area boundaries + single merger break) → 20-25 (mayor context + manual oversight break) → 25-30 (shared files break, Dolt upgrade) → 30+ (you've rebuilt Gas Town)\n\n## TOOLS EXECUTED\nKey tool calls this session (not exhaustive):\n- Read all three core docs (crysknife-design.md, gastown.md, gas-town-user-manual.md) for full context\n- `kiro-cli --help`, `kiro-cli --help-all`, `kiro-cli agent --help`, `kiro-cli mcp --help`, `kiro-cli agent list`, `kiro-cli agent create --help`, `kiro-cli mcp add --help` — discovered kiro-cli native features\n- Read `~/.kiro/agents/agent_config.json.example`, `~/.kiro/settings/mcp.json`, `~/.kiro/settings/cli.json` — understood config format\n- Introspect queries for hooks documentation and context/resources documentation — got full feature docs\n- Multiple str_replace operations on both docs for terminology updates and new sections\n- sed commands for bulk template updates (`crys done` → `@crysknife/done`)\n- grep/wc verification passes after each batch of changes\n\n## NEXT STEPS\n1. **Review multi-agent-progression.md** — User asked if the rest of the document is okay, if anything is missing or can be trimmed. This was the active request when the checkpoint was created. Need to re-read the full doc and assess.\n2. **Create the Crysknife repo** — Move the three docs to a new project folder and start implementation\n3. **Start implementation Phase 1** — `crys init`, `crys start`, `crys stop`, `crys status`, state file management, tmux session creation with worktrees and symlinks, agent config generation, heartbeat, my-task\n\n## TODO LIST\nID if loaded: none\n\n## FACTUAL RECORD\n\n### Files Modified and Read\n_(Most frequently and recently accessed first. Indented items show summaries for the last 5 modifications.)_\n* /Users/lemi/Documents/code/projects/chip8-emulator/docs/crysknife-design.md (17 modifications, 29 reads, showing summaries for last 5 modifications)\n  - Update Done Detection to use MCP tool as primary method\n  - Update quick template done reference\n  - Update mayor template to use MCP tools instead of CLI commands\n  - Update Data Flow section to reflect heartbeat-based monitoring and agent config regeneration\n  - Update System Overview diagram with hooks dir and agent configs\n* /Users/lemi/Documents/code/projects/chip8-emulator/docs/multi-agent-progression.md (16 modifications, 9 reads, showing summaries for last 5 modifications)\n  - Update workflow tiers naming in concepts table\n  - Fix Stage 7 description to say 3-6 agents with Crysknife\n  - No change needed, already correct\n  - Update stage overview with correct agent counts\n  - Rewrite Stage 7 with kiro-cli integration, scaling chart, updated sections\n* /Users/lemi/.kiro/agents/agent_config.json.example\n\n### Commands Executed\n_(Showing 20 of 21 commands, most frequently and recently executed first)_\n* grep -n '3-6 agents' /Users/lemi/Documents/code/projects/chip8-emulator/docs/multi-agent-progression.md - Check for any remaining old 3-6 references in Stage 7\n* wc -l /Users/lemi/Documents/code/projects/chip8-emulator/docs/multi-agent-progression.md - Check final line count\n* grep -n '^## ' /Users/lemi/Documents/code/projects/chip8-emulator/docs/multi-agent-progression.md - Check top-level section structure\n* grep -c '## ' /Users/lemi/Documents/code/projects/chip8-emulator/docs/crysknife-design.md - Count sections to verify structure\n* grep -n 'Run: `crys done\\|run `crys done\\|\\.orchestra' /Users/lemi/Documents/code/projects/chip8-emulator/docs/crysknife-design.md - Check for any remaining old patterns\n* sed -i '' 's/When fully done, run `crys done {{AGENT_ID}}` to notify mayor and merger/When fully done, call @crysknife\\/done to notify mayor and merge... - Update remaining template references\n* sed -i '' 's/- \\[ \\] Run: `crys done {{AGENT_ID}}`/- [ ] Call @crysknife\\/done to notify mayor and merger/' /Users/lemi/Documents/code/projects/chip8-... - Update all template references from crys done to MCP tool\n* find ~/.kiro -name \"*.json\" -o -name \"*.md\" -o -name \"*.yaml\" -o -name \"*.yml\" -o -name \"*.toml\" 2>/dev/null | head -30 - Find kiro config files to understand agent format\n* ls -la ~/.kiro/agents/ 2>/dev/null; echo \"---\"; ls -la ~/Documents/code/projects/chip8-emulator/.kiro/agents/ 2>/dev/null - Check agent config directories\n* kiro-cli agent create --help 2>&1 - Check agent creation options\n* kiro-cli mcp add --help 2>&1 - Check MCP server add options\n* kiro-cli agent list 2>&1 - List existing agents\n* kiro-cli agent --help 2>&1 - Check agent subcommand help\n* kiro-cli mcp --help 2>&1 - Check MCP subcommand help\n* kiro-cli --help-all 2>&1 - Get full help output\n* kiro-cli --help 2>&1 | head -40 - Check kiro-cli top-level help\n* grep -n 'orchestra' /Users/lemi/Documents/code/projects/chip8-emulator/docs/multi-agent-progression.md -
